title: lepidosperma quadrangulatum
intro: Lepidosperma quadrangulatum, also known as the square-fruited sundew, is a species of aquatic plant in the family Restionaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and reduce pain and swelling. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic to combat infections. In herbal preparations, the square-fruited sundew is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
